assumptions: out [7:0]: 8-bit output representing the current state of the ring counter. Only one bit is set high at any time, and the set bit cycles through the 8-bit output.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
Exactly one bit of out is high at any observed clock edge
The high bit moves to the next position on each clock edge without skipping
cases.goal: Verify that the ring counter advances one position per clock after reset.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
No assumption about fixed latency; progress is checked over a conservative number of cycles.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Release reset and observe out for a few clock cycles to confirm deterministic progression
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe over at least three consecutive clock edges after reset release

expected_properties: out returns to the initial state (LSB = 1) after reset is deasserted
No metastability or X-propagation is observed on out after reset release
cases.goal: Validate correct handling of an asynchronous reset assertion/deassertion relative to the clock.
notes: Reset polarity is taken from contract (active high). Timing of deassertion is left ambiguous.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
preconditions: reset signal is present in the contract
stimulus_intent: Assert reset for a single clock cycle, then release it
Observe out for several cycles to confirm it stabilizes to the expected start state
timing_assumptions: Allow at least two clock edges after reset release before checking out stability

expected_properties: out cycles through all eight positions exactly once before repeating
Only one bit of out is high at any clock edge
cases.goal: Demonstrate the ring counter completes a full 8‑state cycle after reset.
notes: Observation window is conservative; exact latency per transition is not assumed.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
preconditions: Reset has been released and the design is in a stable post‑reset state
stimulus_intent: Run the design for at least nine clock cycles and record out values
timing_assumptions: Observe over a minimum of nine consecutive clock edges
